summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined Ukraine's development of the Gnome-NS ground "mother drone" for carrying FPVs, the Ptashka fiber-optic strike quadcopter that flies on an invisible glass thread, and the Shrike Fiber drone winning top place in a Pentagon procurement program. Reports detail a Russian FPV drone strike on an ambulance in Mykolaiv, Ukraine's Chipa net gun for intercepting FPVs at 25 metres, and the T-80BVM tank surviving 18 FPV drone strikes. Ukraine has also unveiled the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone, while the U.S. Army National Guard has purchased small FPV strike drones, and the Pentagon is considering Ukrainian interceptor drones to counter Iran.
